Quality Education.reason: The course provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and explicitly teaches 'Health literacy' (健康識能), a key skill for promoting sustainable development and well-being (Target 4.7).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Directly supports Target 8.3 (entrepreneurship, job creation) through its focus on 'Health Industry Development' and the multi-week 'Business Model Canvas' (創業九宮格) module.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Focuses on 'Health Industry Development' and innovation via the 'Business Model Canvas' (創業九宮格), aligning with Target 9.2 (sustainable industrialization) and 9.3 (support for enterprises).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Addresses the needs of the elderly ('老人'), a vulnerable group, supporting Target 10.2 (social inclusion irrespective of age). The topic 'Patient autonomy' also promotes equal opportunity.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Links to Target 11.1 and 11.2 (access to basic services for older persons) through topics like 'community hospice' (社區安寧) and utilizing 'social resources'.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The topic 'Patient autonomy' (病人自主權) directly supports Target 16.3 by promoting rights and equal access to justice within the healthcare system.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Emphasizes interdisciplinary collaboration, aligning with Target 17.16. Objectives state the need to 'cooperate with other professional fields' and foster 'interaction with interdisciplinary teachers and students'.
